items.title: SK Hynix Shares Drop in Seoul After Much-Hyped US Trading Debut
items.description: SK Hynix Inc. shares declined in Seoul on Monday after the memory chipmaker’s highly anticipated US trading debut.

items.title: US to Increase Mexico Sugar Imports to 1.15 Million Metric Tons
items.description: The US Department of Agriculture estimates it will import as much as 1.15 million metric tons of Mexican sugar in the 2026-2027 period, according to a statement posted on X by Mexican President Claudi

items.title: Treasury Two-Year Yield Rises to Highest Since 2025 on Oil Gain
items.description: The Treasury two-year yield climbed to its highest level in more than 16 months as renewed tensions in Iran pushed up oil prices, fanning speculation the Federal Reserve will tighten policy to keep in

items.title: US Says It Conducted Fresh Round of Strikes Against Iran
items.description: The US releases videos from unidentified locations, saying its forces completed a third round of strikes against Iran. It prompted retaliatory strikes from Tehran. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Season of Selling to End Philippine Peso Rally, Strategists Say
items.description: The Philippine peso’s relief rally following the initial US-Iran ceasefire is likely to be short-lived as seasonal selling drives the currency to new record lows, strategists say.

items.title: Gold Drops as US-Iran Strikes Revive Inflation, Rate-Hike Risks
items.description: Gold declined after the US and Iran exchanged fresh strikes over the weekend, sending energy prices higher and once more raising the prospects for interest-rate hikes to combat inflation.

items.title: Oil Climbs, Treasuries Drop After US Strikes Iran: Markets Wrap
items.description: Oil rose and Treasuries fell after the US launched another round of strikes against Iran, reviving concern that higher energy prices will keep inflation elevated and interest rates higher for longer.

items.title: Oil Jumps as Conflict Over Hormuz Escalates With Fresh Strikes
items.description: Oil jumped as the US and Iran exchanged fresh strikes, with the sides offering conflicting statements on whether the Strait of Hormuz remains open.

items.title: US Says It Began Launching More Strikes Against Iran at 5pm ET

items.title: Traders Grapple With World That’s Good for Dollar, Bad for Bonds
items.description: What’s good for the US dollar isn’t always good for US bonds — but investors are finding ways to work around it.

items.title: Markets Watch Walsh's First Testimony
